Rubroshorea singkawang is a species of plant in the family Clusiaceae, also known as the sterculiad family, and is native to Borneo. The Rubroshorea singkawang plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which have been associated with several health benefits, including the potential to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Rubroshorea singkawang include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Rubroshorea singkawang include tea, capsules, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
